candidate_problems.text: Identify domains where increased model scale alone appears to improve criticism rather than only proposal bandwidth, and determine whether those cases undermine the claim that the bottleneck is primarily structural.

Specify which architectural components most increase epistemic performance: contradiction detection, invariance testing, decomposition of conjectures, memory of failed explanations, or empirical interfaces.
Determine whether there is a threshold of substrate capability below which no criticism-centered architecture can work, making the architecture-vs-scale contrast less primary than claimed.
